produces.artifact: Narrative cross-references in this spec's own design and detail files to specs/spec-driven-refine/spec.xml:32

requirement.title: docs/guide/downstream-contract.md user-facing guide
requirement.description: A new user-facing guide MUST be created at docs/guide/downstream-contract.md. It MUST describe: (1) the three-workflow-terminus contract (arc-finishing-epic Option 1, arc-dispatching-teammates wrap-up, and user-manual, all ending with arcforge finish --if-last or arcforge finish); (2) the Convention C history — why Revert-after-merge (Finding 6.5 transcript) and Reconcile-after-merge (Finding 2.2 transcript) are retired and Delete-after-sprint (Finding 7.3) is canonical; (3) the PR-based carve-out from fr-sh-002 so PR-only users know to invoke finish manually. The guide MUST live in ships=yes territory per .claude/rules/dev-context.md and MUST NOT contain contributor-only footnotes.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given docs/guide/downstream-contract.md after implementation, When the file is read top-to-bottom, Then it MUST contain explicit sections covering (a) the three workflow termini listed above, (b) Convention C history with reference to the relevant Findings in the gap research doc, and (c) the PR carve-out with manual-finish guidance. design.md §Identifiable Requirements R3.1

Given docs/guide/downstream-contract.md, When its prose is reviewed for audience fit, Then it MUST read as instructions for a fresh arcforge user (ships=yes audience) and MUST NOT contain contributor-only caveats such as "only matters when editing arcforge itself" or references to the local dev plugin-disablement. .claude/rules/dev-context.md §Everything you ship is for users

Given docs/guide/downstream-contract.md, When the Convention C section is read, Then it MUST explicitly name the retired conventions (Revert-after-merge, Reconcile-after-merge) and explain in prose why each is no longer supported, so that users finding older transcripts or commits understand the change. design.md §Scope — "不在 scope 內但值得記錄" note on Convention C

requirement.title: Cross-reference to specs/spec-driven-refine/spec.xml:32
requirement.description: This spec's design doc and spec.xml description MUST explicitly reference specs/spec-driven-refine/spec.xml:32 — the exclusion line that defers Phase 2 downstream migration — and MUST identify sdd-downstream-contract as the formal successor fulfilling that deferred scope. The specs/spec-driven-refine spec itself MUST NOT be modified (out-of-scope per this spec's Excludes), so the cross-reference is narrative only.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given specs/sdd-downstream-contract/spec.xml, When the overview description is read, Then it MUST contain an explicit textual reference to specs/spec-driven-refine/spec.xml:32 and MUST state that this spec is the successor for the deferred Phase 2 scope. design.md §Identifiable Requirements R3.2

Given the repository after implementation, When specs/spec-driven-refine/ is inspected, Then it MUST be byte-identical to its pre-implementation state (no modifications, no new files, no added back-reference), so that the successor relationship is expressed only from this spec's side. design.md §Scope — Excludes (Iteration of spec-driven-refine)

Given docs/guide/downstream-contract.md, When the introductory section is read, Then it MUST also reference specs/spec-driven-refine/spec.xml:32 so users arriving at the guide understand the split between upstream pipeline (stages 1-3) and downstream consumer contract (this spec, stages 4+). design.md §Identifiable Requirements R3.1 + R3.2 together
